_meta.purpose: OARS conversational framework for voice interview and NPC dialogue
_meta.rule: At least 70% of questions should be open-ended. Never ask WHY.

O.name: Open-Ended Questions
O.definition: Questions that cannot be answered with yes/no — invite exploration.
O.rule: Use WHAT, WHERE, HOW, WHEN, WHO, WHAT IF. Never WHY.
O.why_not_why: WHY is perceived as challenging or provocative and generates defensiveness.
game_examples: What matters most to you in a game?
What kind of world does your character want to live in?
How does your character feel about what just happened?
What would your character do if they could do anything right now?
What's the most interesting thing your character could do right now?

A.name: Affirmations
A.definition: Statements recognizing strength, effort, and courage — not generic praise.
game_examples: It takes real courage to share that.
Your character just did something worth remembering.
You've been really engaged with this story.
Your character noticed something important there.

R.name: Reflective Listening
R.definition: Rephrasing to capture implicit emotion or meaning — shows you heard them.

types.simple: Repeating or slightly rephrasing what was said.
types.complex: Adding the implied feeling or meaning behind what was said.

game_examples: So it sounds like your character has been carrying this alone for a while.
It sounds like that part of the story felt heavy.
What I'm hearing is that trust is really important to your character.
It seems like your character is looking for a place where they belong.

S.name: Summarizing
S.definition: Linking what was said to check understanding before continuing.
game_examples: What I'm hearing is... Does that feel right?
So far, your character values courage and connection. Is there anything I'm missing?
Let me make sure I understand — your character wants a world where...

curiosity_stance.principle: Therapeutic curiosity — genuine, non-agenda-driven — transforms conversation into shared exploration.
rules: Wait for a topic to organically emerge before probing deeper.
If no follow-up question feels natural, continue listening.
Questions should arise from what the user just said, not from a pre-set checklist.
